2604.05702.title: Dialogue Act Patterns in GenAI-Mediated L2 Oral Practice: A Sequential Analysis of Learner-Chatbot Interactions
2604.05702.paper_summary: While generative AI (GenAI) voice chatbots offer scalable opportunities for second language (L2) oral practice, the interactional processes related to learners' gains remain underexplored. This study investigates dialogue act (DA) patterns in interactions between Grade 9 Chinese English as a foreign language (EFL) learners and a GenAI voice chatbot over a 10-week intervention. Seventy sessions from 12 students were annotated by human coders using a pedagogy-informed coding scheme, yielding 6,957 coded DAs. DA distributions and sequential patterns were compared between high- and low-progress sessions. At the DA level, high-progress sessions showed more learner-initiated questions, whereas low-progress sessions exhibited higher rates of clarification-seeking, indicating greater comprehension difficulty. At the sequential level, high-progress sessions were characterised by more frequent prompting-based corrective feedback sequences, consistently positioned after learner responses, highlighting the role of feedback type and timing in effective interaction. Overall, these findings underscore the value of a dialogic lens in GenAI chatbot design, contribute a pedagogy-informed DA coding framework, and inform the design of adaptive GenAI chatbots for L2 education.
2604.05702.paper_summary_zh: 雖然生成式人工智慧（GenAI）語音聊天機器人為第二語言（L2）口語練習提供了可擴展的機會，但與學習者獲益相關的互動過程仍未被充分探索。本研究調查了第九年級中文英語作為外語（EFL）學習者與GenAI語音聊天機器人之間互動的對話行為（DA）模式，為期10週的介入研究。來自12名學生的70個會話由人類編碼員使用以教學為基礎的編碼方案進行標註，產生了6,957個編碼的DA。在DA層面，高進展會話顯示出更多學習者主動提出的問題，而低進展會話則顯示出更高的澄清需求率，這表明理解困難更大。在序列層面，高進展會話的特徵是更頻繁的基於提示的糾正反饋序列，這些序列始終位於學習者回應之後，突顯了反饋類型和時機在有效互動中的重要性。總體而言，這些發現強調了在GenAI聊天機器人設計中採用對話視角的價值，貢獻了一個以教學為基礎的DA編碼框架，並為L2教育的自適應GenAI聊天機器人的設計提供了信息。
2604.05702.author: Liqun He et.al.
2604.05702.authors: Liqun He, Shijun, Chen, Mutlu Cukurova, Manolis Mavrikis
